title: phaeomeria ciliata
intro: Phaeomeria ciliata, also known as the red salvia, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats. Phaeomeria ciliata can be prepared as a tea or infused in water to utilize its herbal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
